programmes.show_description: Ford Cosworth/Morgan 4/4: Dave goes to Wales to bag a Sierra Cosworth, a sought-after car in the bidding market. Dad Derek gets his hands on a more sedate 1926 Standard Tourer.
Bentley S3/BSA 3-wheeler: Derek heads to Suffolk to collect two Bentleys with a majestic pedigree; Cynthia a stunning 1963 S3 and Harry, a 1965 Continental.
Dodge Bus/VX2300 Police Car: Dave heads to West Yorkshire for a 1926 Dodge bus. Derek gets his hands on a rare Vauxhall Victor VX 90 police car expected to sell for more than a few coppers.
Shopping from Home.
Morpeth to Amble: Michael Portillo explores the wild and rugged coastline of Northumberland by rail, beginning in Morpeth.
Alnmouth to Bamburgh: Michael Portillo travels the length of the Northumbrian coastline, and visits the mighty Bamburgh Castle.
Farne Islands to Berwick-upon-Tweed: Michael Portillo's rail trek along the coast of Northumberland continues towards the Farne Islands.
Fairlie to Port Glasgow: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip from the Firth of Clyde to Lewis and Harris.
Windsor to Godalming: While exploring the Wey and Godalming Navigations, Robbie gets caught in a storm and falls off his narrowboat. He also uncovers rock history in Guildford.
Byfleet to Odiham Castle: Robbie's narrowboat gets stuck under a low bridge on the challenging Basingstoke Canal as he battles through water weed. Can he make it to journey's end?

programmes.show_name: Man and Beast with Martin Clunes
programmes.show_description: Comedy actor Martin Clunes travels to Nepal, where he meets a farmer whose cows not only provide milk, but also the fuel for lighting and cooking in his home.

programmes.show_name: Man and Beast with Martin Clunes
programmes.show_description: Martin Clunes meets doting dog owners in Tokyo, learns the truth behind snake-charming in Nepal and finds out how birds are used for gambling in Thailand.
Islands: Wildlife documentary series presented by David Attenborough, beginning with a look at the remote islands which offer sanctuary to some of the planet's rarest creatures.
Mountains: The wildlife documentary series with David Attenborough continues with a unique and intimate glimpse into the secretive lives of mountain-dwelling animals.
Jungles: Jungles provide the richest habitats on the planet - mysterious worlds of high drama where extraordinary animals attempt to survive in the most competitive places.
Deserts: The world's deserts force animals to come up with ingenious ways of coping with hostile conditions, giving rise to the most incredible survival stories on earth.
Grasslands: Wildlife documentary series presented by David Attenborough. A look at survival in grasslands, from Asia's saiga antelope to the giant anteaters of Brazil.
Cities: Cities can be worlds of opportunity for animals, from the leopards prowling the streets of Mumbai to the peregrine falcons hunting amongst New York's skyscrapers.
Islands: Wildlife documentary series presented by David Attenborough, beginning with a look at the remote islands which offer sanctuary to some of the planet's rarest creatures.
Paul Merton and Suki Webster arrive in Canterbury to pick up their cruiser and set off on their first-ever staycation on wheels touring Kent's coastline.
Paul and Suki negotiate their motorhome under a low bridge in the rain in the Lake District after having a cup of coffee.
VW Barndoor Samba: Irv is an expert in VW Sambas, so when a collector with a bunch of rusted panels and the dream of a showroom-quality vehicle needs help, there's only one man for the job.
Rolls Royce Phantom/VW Bettle: Derek's charmed by a 1929 Rolls Royce Phantom with an unusual history, involving a WW1 hero and a tale of fraud.
Lancia Fulvia/Rover P5B Motorhome: Dave heads to Bolton to pick up a classic Italian sports car - a Lancia Fulvia 1.3 Sport styled by Milan coachbuilder Zagato.

Morgan Plus 8/Clenet Roadster: Derek rubs shoulders with pop music royalty when he picks up a Morgan sports car; a WWII ambulance arrives at Thornton le Dale and a Ford Model T needs a new home. S5 Ep
Audi Quattro/Sunbeam Alpine: The Mathewsons welcome a 1982 Audi Quattro and a Sunbeam Alpine, and Derek takes delivery of a beautiful green Renault van previously used to carry bread in Paris.
Austin Gipsy / Toyota MR2 Ferrari: Derek heads to Scotland in a rainstorm to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible, and Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock-up.
Shopping from Home.
Helensburgh to Connel: Michael Portillo continues his exploration of Scotland's spectacular west coast, travelling from the town of Helensburgh to Connel Bridge.
Oban to Staffa: Onboard the West Highland Line, Michael Portillo explores the stunning scenery of Scotland's west coast and islands as he heads to the Hebridean archipelago.
Fort William to Glenfinnan: Michael Portillo's adventure reaches the west coast of Scotland where, aboard the Jacobite Express, he crosses the awe-inspiring Glenfinnan Viaduct.
Mallaig to Isle of Lewis and Harris: Michael Portillo reaches the terminus of the West Highland Line at Mallaig.
Willys Jeep: Tucked away in the showroom is a WW2-era Willys Jeep. Its original condition means there might be a bunfight for a military vehicle that's ripe for restoration.
Austin Healey: Up for auction this week is a 1980 Ford Escort, a hand-built 1946 Bedford 12 electric car, and a beautiful, red 1957 Austin Healey.
The Kamikaze Bullet: Japan would create arguably the greatest fighter in WW2 - the Mitsubishi Zero. But their failed War Factories would ultimately reduce the Zero to a Kamikaze plane.
The First Six Months in Power: Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation in parliament, but a fire in the Reichstag, blamed on communists, prompts left-wing arrests.
Crathes Castle 2: Fiona Bruce presents from Crathes Castle, Aberdeenshire, where finds include a collection of signed Harry Potter books and a baseball cap with an unusual story to tell.
Cromer Pier 2: Fiona Bruce presents from Cromer seafront in Norfolk, where finds on display include a goblet that survived the Great Fire of London and a sketch that may be by LS Lowry.
Consort Bike/Coble Boat/Volvo: Paul's on the East Coast to help a collector who is saying goodbye to his prized Excelsior Consort and his handmade Yorkshire Coble boat.
Cosworth/Ice Cream Van: Dave is off to London where a couple of very special Fords, including a pristine Escort Cosworth, are hidden away in a garage.
The Kamikaze Bullet: Japan would create arguably the greatest fighter in WW2 - the Mitsubishi Zero. But their failed War Factories would ultimately reduce the Zero to a Kamikaze plane.
The First Six Months in Power: Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation in parliament, but a fire in the Reichstag, blamed on communists, prompts left-wing arrests.
Crathes Castle 2: Fiona Bruce presents from Crathes Castle, Aberdeenshire, where finds include a collection of signed Harry Potter books and a baseball cap with an unusual story to tell.
In the second leg of his journey, Simon Reeve travels down the Rocky Mountains and meets a former Silicon Valley executive who has envisaged a new future for the land in Montana.
Cornish newcomer Dom Chinea tries to fix a leaky sea pool near Mousehole. He buys a battered old Land Rover and starts a new project crushing waste concrete to fill potholes on his lane.
Sri Lanka to Bangladesh: Simon finds out about battles in Sri Lanka, then moves on to Bangladesh, where he hears about the fast-growing industry providing prawns for the West.
Consort Bike/Coble Boat/Volvo: Paul's on the East Coast to help a collector who is saying goodbye to his prized Excelsior Consort and his handmade Yorkshire Coble boat.
Cosworth/Ice Cream Van: Dave is off to London where a couple of very special Fords, including a pristine Escort Cosworth, are hidden away in a garage.
